original: Walls of upper city of Jerusalem battered down by Roman army
Earthquake in the Gulf of Chihli (Bohai Sea) near China reportedly kills 100,000 people
Duke of Brabant John II signs the Charter of Kortenberg creating a Parliament of Kortenberg (The Lords of Kortenberg)
Battle of Płowce: Kingdom of Poland defeats the Teutonic Order in a strategic victory
Pope Paul III officially recognizes the Society of Jesus (Jesuit) co-founded by Ignatius of Loyola as a religious order of the Catholic church
Pope Urban VII dies 13 days after being chosen as Pope, making his reign the shortest papacy in history
Dutch Moluccan fleet of the Fifth Expedition, under command of Wolfert Harmensz, land on Mauritius suffering from scurvy, where they hunt and document the dodo
The armies of Sweden are utterly defeated by the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth in the Battle of Kircholm
Hurricane hits Carlisle Bay Barbados; 27 British ships sink & 3,000 die
French founder of Detroit Antoine de Lamothe Cadillac is imprisoned in the Bastille for speaking “against the government of the state and the colonies ” (freed 1718)
French troops conquer Dutch fortress Fort Crèvecoeur after a short siege
Dutch army leaves Brussels, after hundreds killed

French fishing vessel SS Vesta collides with American passenger paddle-wheel ship SS Arctic off Newfoundland in heavy fog, sinking the larger passenger ship; 322 killed, most of the survivors were crew members
George Frederick Bristow's "Rip Van Winkle", 2nd American opera, opens in NYC
Jo Shelby's cavalry in action at Moffat's Station, Arkansas
Battle at Pilot Knob (Ft Davidson) Missouri: 1700 killed or injured
Circus performer James Cooke walks a 150 yard tightrope from Cliff House to Seal Rocks in San Francisco
John Mercer Langston named minister of Haiti
Chicago Cubs beat Troy 10-8 before record small "crowd" of 12
Book matches are patented by Diamond Match Company
Aqueduct racetrack opens in NY
Elephantine Colossus, a vacant seven-story building in the shape of an elephant built in 1885, burns to the ground in Coney Island, New York
Victory Theater (Republic, Belasco) opens at 207 W 42nd St NYC
Wreck of the Old 97, a train crash made famous by the song of the same name.
Boston's Bill Dinneen no-hits Chicago White Sox, 2-0
1st test flight of a twin-engined airplane (France)
Cleveland 2nd baseman Nap Lajoie collects his 3,000th hit
1st Native American Day celebrated, honoring American Indians
Emperor Lidj Jasu of Ethiopia flees a palace coup led by his aunt Zauditu
Broadhurst Theatre, designed by Herbert J. Krapp for the Shubert brothers, opens at 235 W 44th Street, NYC
British troops withdraw from the North Russian town of Archangelsk after fighting Bolsheviks/Soviets
Democratic National Committee votes to allow female members
Pitcher Bob Shawkey sets then Yank record with 15 strike-outs
American swimmer Johnny Weismuller sets his first 2 world records in the 100m and 150yd freestyle events the A.A.U. Nationals meet in Brighton Beach, NY
MLB NY Yankees beat Cleveland Indians 21-7 at Polo Grounds, NYC
Italian troops leave occupied Corfu
Giants clinch their 4th straight pennant, beating Phils 5-1
The Nationalist Republic of China is recognised by the United States.
MLB'sLou Gehrigcompletes his 6th straight season, playing in every game (.3486) Jim Bottomley (.3481)
Chicago Cubs win 21st consecutive game & clinch NL pennant
Boston Redskins and Brooklyn Dodgers play one of only 4 penalty free games in NFL history; Boston wins 14-3 at Ebbets Field, Brooklyn
Netherlands leave Gold Standard/devaluate guilder
1st Santa Claus Training School opens (Albion NY)
Balinese Tiger declared extinct
British ocean liner "Queen Elizabeth" launches at Clydebank, Scotland
Jewish lawyers forbidden to practise in Germany
League of Nations declares Japan the aggressor against China
Warsaw surrenders to Germans after 19 days of resistance
White Sox host 1st "day-night" doubleheader, lose twice to Cleveland, 5-2 & 7-5
55 German aircraft shot down above England
Black leaders protest discrimination in US armed forces
Nazi Germany, Italy & Japan sign 10 year formal alliance (Axis)
Yankees, lose to A's & eliminated from pennant for 1st time since 1935
Heavy German assault in Stalingrad
NY Giants beat Wash Redskins 14-7 without making a 1st down
St Louis Cards win NL pennant on last day of season
Anti-fascism opposition begins in Naples
Dutch opposition newspaper "The Slogan" publishes KZ-Lower letter
Dutch cities Helmond and Oss are liberated
King George II of Greece returns from exile to Athens
Persian troops occupy oil refinery at Abadan
Baltimore Colt Bert Rechichar kicks a 56-yard field goal

Braves & Dodgers finish in a tie (86-68)
Europe's 1st "moving pavement" (travelator), opens at Bank station on the London Underground

Military uprising under Col Abdullah as-Sallal North Yemen
US sells Israel Hawk anti-aircraft missiles
At 10:59 AM census clock, records US population at 190,000,000

Despite 3 HRs by Johnny Callison, Phils are 14-8 losers to Braves
Phillies 7th straight loses sends them into 2nd place
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site

Cardinals' super pitcher Bob Gibson's 13th shutout of year
France denies UK entry into common market
Ken Boswell sets 2nd baseman record of 85 games without an error

1st NHL exhibition game at Nassau Coliseum; NY Rangers beat NY Islanders, 6-4

-10/26] Rome: 4th bishop synod

US Congress' final approval to create Department of Education

Marvin Hagler defeats Alan Minter to claim boxing's world Middleweight championship in London. They have to be escorted away by police after a riot forms.

Cards clinching NL East title
Constantine Huygens Prize is awarded to novelist Jan Wolkers, nut he declines to accept it
John Palmer becomes news anchor of Today Show

8 killed as roof collapses in Brussel's supermarket
Hurricane Gloria's 130 MPH wind hits Atlantic coast
"Give Me Wings" single released by Michael Johnson (Billboard Song of the Year 1987)

NFL players' strike begins in the US

Oakland wins AL West & SF wins NL West title

Timor-Leste (East Timor) joins the United Nations

Atlanta Braves clinch their 14th straight division title thanks to Philadelphia's loss to the New York Mets

The 2004 Japanese discovery of the 133th element has been confirmed

19 people are killed and 45 are injured in a bus bombing in Peshawar, Pakistan
60 people are killed after a building collapses in Mumbai, India

At least 22 people are killed after a refugee ship capsizes off the island of Java

John Adamsis appointed to negotiate Revolutionary War peace terms with Great Britain
Battle of Bussaco:Arthur Wellesley's Anglo-Portuguese Army defeats a larger French force led by Marshal André Masséna with the French suffering 4,500 dead and wounded

Charles X, King of France, makes his state entry into Paris to popular acclaim
George Stephenson's "Locomotion No. 1" becomes the first steam locomotive to carry passengers on a public rail line, the Stockton and Darlington Railway in England

Centralia Massacre: Pro-Confederate guerrillas led byWilliam T. AndersonwithJesse Jamesloot Centralia and attack a train on the North Missouri Railroad, executing 24 Union soldiers. Later the same day the guerillas defeat the newly formed 39th Missouri Infantry Regiment, killing 123 of its 147 solders.
Czech composerAntonín Dvořákbecomes Director of the National Conservatory of Music of America, in New York City; remains until spring of 1895
